name: ZB (Zborowski-Bruchem)
description: An advanced extension of CFOP created by Zbigniew Zborowski and Ron van Bruchem. The ZB method modifies the last F2L pair insertion to simultaneously orient last layer edges (ZBF2L), then solves the entire last layer in one look using ZBLL. This eliminates the OLL step entirely and achieves a two-look solve after cross. While extremely algorithm-heavy (over 800 algorithms total), ZB represents the theoretical optimum for a cross-based method.

steps.name: Cross
steps.description: Solve the four bottom-layer edge pieces to form a cross, identical to the CFOP cross step.
tips: Plan the entire cross during inspection
Solve on the bottom to look ahead to F2L
Cross efficiency is critical for competitive times

steps.name: First Three F2L Pairs
steps.description: Solve three of the four corner-edge pairs in the first two layers using standard F2L techniques.
tips: Use standard intuitive or algorithmic F2L
Plan pair tracking during cross solve
Leave the easiest slot for the ZBF2L step

steps.name: ZBF2L (Last Pair + Edge Orientation)
steps.description: Insert the last F2L pair while simultaneously orienting all last layer edges. This step replaces the normal last pair insertion and eliminates the need for OLL. There are 306 ZBF2L algorithms covering every case.
tips: WV and VLS are subsets that cover common cases
Recognition requires tracking both the pair and LL edge orientation

steps.name: ZBLL (Last Layer)
steps.description: Solve the entire last layer in one algorithm. Since all edges are already oriented from ZBF2L, only ZBLL cases apply (493 algorithms). This completes the solve in a single look.
tips: Learn one subset at a time
COLL + EPLL is a simpler alternative

pros: Only two looks after cross (last pair + last layer)
cons: Enormous time investment to learn fully
Very few solvers have mastered the complete set
Diminishing returns compared to standard CFOP + ZBLL
creator: Zbigniew Zborowski & Ron van Bruchem
